title: calocephalus lacteus
intro: Calocephalus lacteus, also known as milkflower or white button, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and small, button-like appearance. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Calocephalus lacteus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
